sections.paragraph:
(Arabic sing. مَعْىُ) only in plural, (A), מִעִים constr. מְעֵי, with suff. מֵעֶיךָ, and also מֵעֵיהֶם (for מְעֵיהֶם), Eze 7:19

(1) intestines, יָצָא מִמְּעֵי פ׳ to come forth from any one’s bowels, to be descended from any one, Gen 15:4 ( 25:23 ); 2Sa 7:12 16:11 .

(2) the belly, Jon 2:1 specially used of the womb. Gen 25:23 Rth 1:11, הַעֹוד־לִי בָנִים בְּמֵעַי “are there yet sons in my womb?” מִמְּעֵי אִמִּי “from my mother’s womb,” Isa 49:1 Psa 71:6. Once used of the external belly, Son 5:14 (comp. Dan 2:32).

(3) the breast, the heart, figuratively, the inmost soul. Job 30:27 Lam 1:20 Son 5:4 Isa 16:11 Psa 40:9, תֹּורָֽתְךָ בְּתֹוךְ מֵעַי “thy law is in the midst of my bowels,” i.e. set deeply in my soul. Compare רַחֲמִים.

(B) מֵעֹות the bowels. So in the difficult passage, Isa 48:19, which I would thus explain, “thy seed shall be as the sand, וְצֶאֱצָאֵי מֵעֶיךָ כִּמְעֹותָיו and the offspring of thy bowels like (that of) its bowels,” sc. of the sea (verse 18 Isa 48:18); for the more full כְּצֶאֱצָאֵי מְעֹותָיו, i.e. as the fishes of the sea sprung up in its bowels. מֵעֹות is i.q. מֵעִים, but the feminine form indicates a figurative use. Ill rendered by the ancient versions כִּמְעֹותָיו as its small stones, sc. of sand, which is commonly defended by comparing Chaldee מְעָא a small coin, obolus (perhaps a small stone), and the Arabic مَاغِيَّةُ, which denotes a scruple of conscience, not a small stone.
